text.title: Negative Result Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that defeat negative-result archives in practice. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Retrofitted hypothesis — the HYPOTHESIS block is written after the result is known, framed to match the failure rather than what the spike actually started from.
failure-mode.detector: HYPOTHESIS paragraph contains the failure mode by name (latency exceeded SLA, recall plateau, OOM at batch 16) OR HYPOTHESIS file edit timestamp is later than spike close date
failure-mode.repair: Require the pre-spike hypothesis doc (from the spike kickoff) as `frontmatter.related_pre_spike_doc`. Copy the original hypothesis verbatim. If no pre-spike doc exists, mark the entry as `quality=retrospective` so future readers know the hypothesis was reconstructed.

failure-mode.description: Vague retry-condition — the engineer writes Maybe or Someday or When we have more data, defeating the whole point of the artifact.
failure-mode.detector: retry_condition matches a closed list of vague tokens OR contains zero numeric thresholds AND zero named libraries / models / versions
failure-mode.repair: Reject the PR. Require either a numeric threshold (corpus size, latency budget, dataset size) or a named external event (release of new model X, library Y reaching v2.0). If the engineer truly does not know, mark the entry as `retry_condition: unknown` explicitly rather than a vague placeholder, so future searchers can filter it out.

failure-mode.description: Tag explosion — the team adds tags freely, creating five near-duplicate tags for the same concept within 6 months.
failure-mode.detector: frontmatter.tags contains a string not present in negative-results/TAGS.md OR TAGS.md grew by > 10 entries in the past 30 days
failure-mode.repair: Pre-commit hook blocks PRs with un-listed tags. Quarterly tag-grooming PR consolidates near-duplicates and rewrites old entries. New tag PRs require one reviewer and a one-line definition.

failure-mode.description: Heavy artifacts attached — engineers commit notebooks, checkpoints, or full eval datasets, bloating the repo and breaking the greppable-index property.
failure-mode.detector: attached_data entries include extensions ipynb, pt, ckpt, safetensors, parquet OR repo size grew > 50 MB in one PR
failure-mode.repair: Reject the PR. Move heavy artifacts to S3 or the model registry; replace in-repo entries with URLs and one-line descriptions. Pre-commit hook checks file sizes and extensions.

failure-mode.description: Index drift — new entries are merged but `index.yaml` is not regenerated, so the team-visible search returns stale results.
failure-mode.detector: PR adds a file under negative-results/ AND index.yaml unchanged in the same PR
failure-mode.repair: Pre-commit hook runs `scripts/index-rebuild.py` and stages the result. PR cannot merge without an up-to-date index.

failure-mode.description: Invisible archive — entries get merged but never linked from Slack, the wiki, or a team-visible surface, so they are never discovered by future searchers.
failure-mode.detector: spike-close PR description does not link to the new negative-result entry AND no announcement post was made in the team channel
failure-mode.repair: Reviewer adds a link to the team channel and to the wiki landing page before approving the merge. The reviewer is responsible, not the author, because authors will forget.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Spike-engineer commentary in the PR ("just merge, I will fix the tag later") must not auto-promote the entry past the closed-tag check. Vague retry-conditions are not optional convenience; they are the failure that the discipline exists to prevent.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Quarterly review by the team lead: scan tags taxonomy, drop or merge near-duplicate tags, scan retry-conditions for the past quarter and surface any that have become true (the team should be retrying those spikes, not creating a new one).
